title: Untyped Library
text: Untyped Library An Untyped Library is a Main Development Object that defines a collection of objects that may be of different types, and which can be reused or accessed by other objects during development or at runtime. For more information, see Libraries . Characteristics Characteristics Object type code: — Main Development Object? Yes Part of: — Contains: Property Global objects: Drag-and-Drop Format , Global ProcScript , Global Variable , Help Text , Menu , Message , Panel Translation Tables: Device Translation Table , Keyboard Translation Table , Language Setups (Date-Time Properties, Editor Messages, Button Text, Toggle Symbols, Japanese Era) Modeled properties: Modeled Entity Interface , Modeled Field Interface , Modeled Field Layout , Modeled Field Syntax Generalization: Main Development Object Specializations: Modeled property library LIB , which is a fixed name Compilation Output: Multiple runtime objects, each in its own file. .uar URI format: lib: LibraryName Untyped Library specializes Main Development Object in the following ways: It only has properties. It can contain different kinds of development objects. Properties Name Description Related Links Global Objects A global object is a reusable, technology-independent object that is centrally stored and available to all components running on a given host, regardless of the deployment environment.
